headline: How Early‑Year Catastrophe Losses At Allstate (ALL) Have Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In March 2026, The Allstate Corporation reported estimated catastrophe losses for February of US$140 million (US$111 million after tax), bringing total estimated catastrophe losses for January and February to US$315 million (US$249 million after tax), while reiterating its focus on protection and insurance products under its core brands. These early‑year catastrophe losses highlight how exposure to severe weather events can affect near‑term profitability, a key consideration for...
